title: Aspidium laciniatum
intro: Aspidium laciniatum, also known as the lacinate spleenwort, is a species of fern native to Asia, characterized by its lance-shaped fronds with deeply lobed segments. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help alleviate conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin irritations. The bioactive constituents of Aspidium laciniatum include triterpenoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Aspidium laciniatum include infusions, decoctions, and tonics, often used in traditional Chinese medicine to treat a range of health issues.
taxonomy: Aspidium laciniatum is classified in the family Dryopteridaceae. It belongs to the genus Aspidium, which is now considered a synonym for the genus Dryopteris. This species is a type of fern, specifically a member of the leptosporangiate ferns. Further classification places Aspidium laciniatum in the order Polypodiales. The species is native to the tropical and subtropical regions of Asia.
uses: Aspidium laciniatum, commonly known as the split lip fern, has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits. Its extracts have been found to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating wounds and skin infections. The plant is also used to treat respiratory issues such as coughs and bronchitis due to its expectorant properties. Additionally, Aspidium laciniatum has been traditionally used to treat fever and diarrhea. More research is needed to fully understand the medicinal uses of this plant.

parts: Aspidium laciniatum, commonly known as the split-leaf fern, is a plant species used in traditional medicine. Its medicinal parts include the leaves and rhizome, which are used to treat various health conditions. The leaves are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, making them useful for treating wounds and reducing inflammation. The rhizome is used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant properties. The plant is also used to treat fever and other infections.

side_effects: Aspidium laciniatum, also known as the compound fern, is a plant native to parts of Asia and is traditionally used in herbal medicine. However, there is limited information available on its side effects.
